data.title: Swiss Marketplace Group prices IPO at 46 francs per share
data.description:
Swiss Marketplace Group priced shares for its initial public offering at 46 Swiss francs ($58.04) each on Friday, implying a market capitalisation of 4.5 billion Swiss francs, the company said.

The offer price was at the top of the range the online marketplace had given earlier this month for its IPO on Switzerland's SIX exchange.

($1 = 0.7925 Swiss francs)

data.title: China's yuan holds steady ahead of Trump-Xi call
data.description:
China's yuan held steady against the dollar on Friday, even as the central bank set its midpoint at a more than three-week low, with investors waiting on leads from a potentially crucial phone call between the leaders of the world's two largest economies.

Trade relations between Washington and Beijing have long been one of the key factors influencing the yuan's value and market sentiment over the past few years, and the call due later in the session could affect the outlook of the Chinese currency.

A framework agreement to switch short-video app TikTok to U.S.-controlled ownership will be confirmed in the call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, while talks about a trade truce is also expected on the agenda.

"Trump and Xi will hold a call tonight, which is expected to formalize an agreement on TikTok and signal a possible easing of trade tensions," said Chang Wei Liang, FX & credit strategist at DBS.

As of 0404 GMT, the onshore yuan was 0.02% higher at 7.1117 per dollar, while its offshore counterpart was down about 0.01% in Asian trade to 7.1101.

Prior to the market's opening, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate at 7.1128 per dollar, its weakest since August 26 and 46 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate of 7.1174. The spot yuan is allowed to trade 2% either side of the fixed midpoint each day.

Investors have been carefully gauging the central bank's daily guidance fix for any subtle changes in the official stance after the Chinese currency gradually rose to the strongest level since November in recent sessions, currency traders and analysts said.

The much weakened yuan midpoint fixing could suggest that the central bank still prefers a measured yuan appreciation, said a trader at a Chinese bank, noting neither rapid appreciation nor depreciation is ideal from the PBOC's perspective.

Separately, a recent sharp stock rally has prompted some global investment houses to revise up their yuan forecasts.

"China A-shares have outperformed the S&P 500 and most Asian indices, but the equity rally in China has thus far been domestically driven," said Abbas Keshvani, Asia macro strategist at RBC Capital Markets.

"If foreigners were to shed their aversion to China stocks and wade in, it could drive yuan outperformance, instead of mere dollar-driven appreciation."

They expect the yuan to trade at 7.08 per dollar at end-2025, and 7.0 at end-2026, versus 7.2 and 7.08, respectively, in a previous prediction.

China's stock market has been on a tear, with the benchmark Shanghai Composite Index hovering near its 10-year highs.

data.title: India bonds flat, urgent oversupply worries trump potential addition to global index
data.description:
By Dharamraj Dhutia

Indian government bonds were caged in a narrow range as traders focussed on present supply concerns instead of the debt's potential inclusion into a global index, a positive action that could spur demand over the medium term.

The yield on the 10-year benchmark (IN063335G=CC) was at 6.5134% as of 10:10 a.m. IST, after ending at 6.5139% on Thursday.

Yields move inversely to prices.

New Delhi will sell the benchmark 2035 bond worth 300 billion rupees ($3.40 billion) later in the day. The auction comes at a time when the market remains concerned about constant supply from the centre and states, which has pushed up yields and skewed demand-supply dynamics.

Traders also await the borrowing calendar for the second half of the fiscal year, which is likely to be published before the end of September.

In a round of consultations with multiple market participants earlier this month, the central bank received suggestions to reduce the share of ultra-long bonds in overall supply and also cut weekly auction sizes.

"Market is choosing to focus on near-term pain which is real instead of reacting to a far-fetched development which may or may not actually materialise," trader with a primary dealership said.

Bloomberg Index Services is seeking views from investors on whether Indian government bonds should be included in its flagship global aggregate index, according to a notice sent to investors, seen by Reuters.

The index provider included India in its emerging market local currency bond index from January 2025.

Meanwhile, hawkish commentary from the U.S. Federal Reserve continued to weigh on sentiment, even as the central bank reduced interest rates and indicated more cuts.

RATES

India's overnight index swaps were mixed, with the shorter end marginally lower, while the longer end witnessing a slight uptick.

The one-year OIS rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was at 5.46% and the two-year OIS rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) was at 5.4450%.

The liquid five-year OIS rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was higher at 5.7225%.

($1 = 88.2400 Indian rupees)

data.title: Bank of Japan Leaves Rates Unchanged, Announces ETF Sales
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--The Bank of Japan left its policy settings unchanged Friday but said it would begin selling stock holdings, marking another step toward normalizing its monetary policy.

The Japanese central bank kept its overnight call rate target at 0.5%, refraining from raising rates as it assesses how U.S. tariffs will affect the economy and waits for domestic political uncertainty to ease.

The central bank has kept rates at that level since its last hike in January.

At the same time, it also announced plans to reduce its holdings of exchange-traded funds, which it had purchased as part of its aggressive monetary-easing program. It said it would sell about 330 billion yen in ETFs annually, based on book value.

While a trade agreement between the U.S. and Japan cleared some doubts about the tariff outlook, the impact on the economy and companies remains unclear.

Japan managed to negotiate a lower tariff rate than what the U.S. had previously threatened, but companies still face a much bigger burden than they did before. That will reduce Japanese firms' competitiveness in the U.S. market and hurt their profits.

Policymakers at the central bank want to see whether Japanese companies will sustain capital investment and wage growth under the new tariff regime. Key indicators include the upcoming tankan corporate sentiment survey and the quarterly BOJ branch managers meeting, which tracks regional economic conditions and wage growth momentum.

A fresh bout of political instability at home could complicate the BOJ's policy path. Economists say the central bank may have to pause rate increases if Japan's new leadership shifts to favoring a more dovish monetary policy.

However, with inflation persistent and living costs rising, a new leader could face limits on pressing the BOJ to keep monetary conditions loose.

Government data released earlier showed overall consumer prices rose 2.7% in August from a year ago, well above the central bank's target of 2%.

Economists remain divided on the outlook. Some expect the BOJ to hold off on rate hikes until next year, while others say it can make a move as soon as October if it becomes confident that prices and wages are on track to keep growing at a healthy pace.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: BOJ holds, with a hawkish twist
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

An expected hold from the Bank of Japan rounded out the central bank bonanza this week that has left stocks ripping higher and the currency market subdued, with the dollar staying soft as investors remain uneasy about the global rates outlook.

The BOJ's decision, though, was not unanimous with two dissents suggesting policymakers are at odds over when to hike next.

Focus will now be on BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda's press conference where markets will look for clues on the rate outlook and more details on the central bank's plans to sell its holdings of ETFs and real-estate investment trusts (REIT).

The yen firmed immediately after the decision although nothing to get out of bed for, while the Nikkei slipped after touching another record high earlier in the session.

Elsewhere, Asian markets tracked Wall Street higher after the Federal Reserve cut rates as expected earlier this week, although comments from Chair Jerome Powell were measured and did not hint at a rapid lowering of borrowing costs.

Just don't tell the stock market that. Taiwan's benchmark index TWSE:TAIEX hit another record high on Friday after Wall Street's main indexes scaled record peaks. European futures indicate a subdued open after a strong session on Thursday.

The Bank for International Settlements, which acts as an umbrella group for the world's central banks, warned this week that record global share prices appear increasingly disconnected from the rising concerns about government debt levels in bond markets.

The dollar has been steady although investors expect the greenback to be weaker in the near term. Bear in mind, the U.S. currency is down more than 10% this year so how much further it falls from here might be limited.

With little economic data on tap during European hours, the spotlight will remain on interest rates and some whipsawed reaction to the BOJ decision through the session.

European tech stocks (.SX8P) will also be in focus after Nvidia said it would pour $5 billion into Intel , throwing its weight behind the struggling U.S. chipmaker.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

Economic events: UK retail sales for August, Germany August producer prices

Maruti Suzuki to hold investor meeting on September 25, 2025
data.description:
Maruti Suzuki India Limited has announced that it will hold a meeting with investors on September 25, 2025.

The meeting is being held pursuant to Regulation 30(6) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

The company has informed the stock exchanges about the scheduled investor meeting.

Note: Date is subject to change. Change may happen due to exigencies on the part of investors/Company.

The company's head office is located at 1, Nelson Mandela Road, Vasant Kunj, New Delhi - 110070, India.The company has plants located in Gurgaon and Manesar, Haryana.

data.title: Euro Pratik Sales IPO allotment today: Steps to check status on BSE, NSE and MUFG Intime India
data.description:
Euro Pratik Sales Ltd, a leading player in the decorative wall panels and laminates industry, is expected to finalise the share allotment status for its Initial Public Offering (IPO) on Friday, September 19.

The ₹451.3-crore mainboard IPO was fully subscribed on the last day of bidding on Thursday. The IPO, which was a complete offer for sale, was open for subscription from September 16 to 18.

By the close of bidding, Euro Pratik Sales IPO was subscribed 1.34 times, with bids for 1.8 crore shares against nearly 1.35 crore shares on offer. The Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) segment was subscribed 1.05 times. The retail quota was booked 1.23 times, while the Non-Institutional Investors (NII) segment was subscribed 1.92 times.

The IPO bidders are now waiting for the share allotment status to be finalised by the company. The investors, who participated in the Euro Pratik Sales IPO subscription, can check the share allotment status on the websites of NSE, BSE and MUFG Intime India, the issue registrar.

How to check IPO Euro Pratik Sales IPO allotment status on BSE

1. Visit the BSE allotment page at https://www.bseindia.com/investors/appli_check.aspx

2. Select ‘Equity’ under issue type.

3. Choose ‘Euro Pratik Sales’ from the dropdown list.

4. Enter your Application Number or PAN.

5. Click ‘Search’ to view allotment details.

Steps to check IPO allotment status on MUFG Intime India

1. Go to MUFG Intime India at https://in.mpms.mufg.com/Initial_Offer/public-issues.html

2. Choose ‘Euro Pratik Sales’ from the dropdown list for company names.

3. Enter your PAN, Application Number, or DP/Client ID.

4. Click on the ‘Submit’ button.

Similarly, the investors can also check the IPO allotment status on the NSE website using their PAN and application number.

Following the finalisation of share allocation, Euro Pratik Sales is expected to initiate refunds and transfer of shares to the Demat accounts on September 22.

Shares of Euro Pratik Sales are tentatively scheduled to be listed on the NSE and BSE on September 23.

About the company

Euro Pratik Sales operates in the decorative wall panel and decorative laminates segment. It is one of India’s largest organised wall panel brands, with a revenue-based market share of 10% in FY24 and 15.9% in FY23. The company specialises in creating innovative design templates for wall panels and laminates, tailored to modern architectural and interior design trends. Its products are marketed under the brands Euro Pratik and Gloirio.

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on Sept 19
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira was quoted at 41.3985 against the U.S. dollar at 0353 GMT, compared to Thursday's close of 41.34.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 1% lower at 11,048.11 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares are set to end a big central bank week in positive territory, buoyed by hopes of more monetary stimulus around the world, while the Nikkei notched a record aided by expectations the Bank of Japan would again skip a rate hike.

ERDOGAN SCHEDULE

President Tayyip Erdogan will address two events in Istanbul, at 1130 and 1400 GMT.

OPPOSITION LEADER

CHP Chairman Ozgur Ozel will attend the hearings of his party's Izmir provincial chair (0630 GMT) and former city mayor, as part of the sprawling crackdown on the opposition.

JUSTICE MINISTER

Minister Yilmaz Tunc will speak at a Conference of Justice Ministers of the Council of Europe, in Malta.

TEKNOFEST

Three government ministers will attend TEKNOFEST, the aviation, space and technology festival in Istanbul.

RELIGIOUS AFFAIRS

The newly-appointed president of religious affairs, Safi Arpagus, will take over from Ali Erbas in a ceremony at the Presidency.

CENTRAL BANK

The central bank said in minutes of its last policy meeting that inflation may rise slightly in September in part due to food and service prices. Also on Thursday it announced its gross FX reserves dipped to $84.5 billion.

TURKISH AIRLINES

Turkish Airlines' THYAO.IS surprise deal for a stake in Spanish carrier Air Europa, outlasting European rivals Lufthansa and Air France-KLM, was in large part down to its willingness to share control of the carrier with the airline's Hidalgo family owners, four sources told Reuters.

CYPRUS

Turkey is monitoring "attempts to disrupt the balance in Cyprus" and will act to safeguard security in the north of the island, a Turkish official said on Thursday after Cypriot reports that the government there had procured an air defence system from Israel.

U.S. DIPLOMATS IN ISTANBUL

Some of the most senior U.S. diplomats focused on Syria have been abruptly let go from their posts in recent days, people familiar with the matter said, a shake-up that comes as Washington seeks to integrate its Syrian Kurdish allies with the central administration in Damascus.

data.title: Vodafone Idea shares gain ahead of Supreme Court hearing on AGR dues
data.description:
Shares of Vodafone Idea Ltd. were trading higher on September 19, ahead of the Supreme Court hearing of the debt-ridden telecom company's plea against the Department of Telecom's (DoT) additional adjusted gross revenue (AGR) demand.

Earlier this week, the Supreme Court Registry assigned September 19 as the date of hearing for Vodafone Idea's AGR plea.

The company petitioned the top court to set aside DoT's demand for ₹9,450 crore in additional AGR dues, arguing that it goes beyond the scope of the Court's previous ruling on AGR liabilities.

As per reports, the DoT filed an affidavit defending its stance, saying the additional dues represent a 'gap' from previous accounting and is not a reassessment. It maintains these liabilities emerged after the completion of financial accounts and were not covered under the Supreme Court's 2019 judgment.

Of the ₹9,450 crore, as much as ₹2,774 crore comprises the FY18-19 dues of the merged entity — Vodafone Idea and Idea group post their August 2018 consolidation. Meanwhile, ₹5,675 crore pertains to the pre-merger Vodafone Group.

Vodafone Idea contested the calculation, claiming some of the amounts were duplicated and sought fresh reconciliation starting from pre-FY17.

Chandra Sekhar Pemmasani, the minister of state for communications, had told CNBC-TV18 that the Centre would not extend any additional relief to the financially-stressed telecom firm.

Pemmasani, referring to the 2021 support package, under which around ₹53,000 crore of dues were converted into equity, giving the Centre a 49% stake, said, "Whatever we wanted to do has already been done. Vodafone is up to their management… they know how to manage and it's up to them to take it forward."

On July 2, telecom minister Jyotiraditya Scindia had also told CNBC-TV18 that the Centre does not have any plans to convert Vodafone Idea into a public sector undertaking (PSU).

Shares of Vodafone Idea were trading 1.4% up at ₹7.95 apiece around 10 am on Friday. The stock has gained 21% in the past month.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

The Bank of Japan's decision to hold rates steady underscores its cautious stance amid slowing inflation and global uncertainty, says Hiroaki Amemiya at Capital Group. "The BOJ is signaling its readiness to respond to external volatility while continuing to assess the strength of Japan's economic recovery," says the investment director. The central bank's strategy supports the early stages of a reflationary cycle. "We expect the yen to gradually strengthen as interest rate differentials narrow--enhancing Japan's purchasing power and supporting domestic demand." Japan's industrials, manufacturing and automobile sectors appear well-positioned to navigate trade headwinds and benefit from global supply chain shifts, Amemiya adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan's Nikkei flips to losses, yen firms after BOJ decision
data.description:
Key points:
  • Japan's central bank keeps policy steady, but in split vote
  • BOJ announces start of ETF, REIT sales
  • Nikkei falls from record high; JGB yields near 17-year peak

By Rocky Swift and Kevin Buckland

Japan's Nikkei share average flipped to losses on Friday and the yen firmed after the Bank of Japan (BOJ) kept interest rates steady as expected, but in a split decision with two of the nine board members voting for a hike.

The central bank also announced it will begin selling its holdings of exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and real-estate investment trusts, amassed over a decade of massive stimulus.

Japanese government bond futures sank, while the yield on 10-year sovereign debt jumped to a nearly 17-year peak.

"It came as a surprise," Hirofumi Suzuki, chief currency strategist at SMBC, said about the BOJ's decision.

"With the start of ETF sales and two dissenting votes against leaving policy unchanged, i.e., in favour of tightening, the outcome was hawkish despite expectations for a straightforward hold."

The focus now falls on BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda's news conference at 0630 GMT.

The Nikkei 225 Index lost 1% to 44,800.40 as of 0410 GMT, not long after the central bank's announcement.

Earlier, it rose as much as 1.2% to a record high of 45,852.75, driven by a surge in chip-sector stocks following an overnight rally in U.S. peers.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) added four basis points (bps) to 1.635%, just short of this month's high of 1.64%, a level not seen since July 2008.

Benchmark 10-year JGB futures (2JGBv1) shed 0.4 yen to 136.16 yen.

Bond yields move inversely to prices.

The yen strengthened as much as 0.5% to 147.27 per dollar , undoing part of the last two sessions' 1% drop.

data.title: Malaysia Growth May Soften on Weaker External Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's GDP growth is projected to moderate to 4.1% in 2026, down from 4.5% expected previously, as external demand softens and consumption growth slows, CIMB economists Michelle Chia and Azri Azhar say in a note. The government is expected to continue fiscal consolidation, narrowing the budget deficit to 3.6% of GDP from an expected 3.8% in 2025, while targeting revenue growth through wider tax coverage, e-invoicing and a potential hike in sin taxes, they say. Fiscal policy should continue supporting growth while staying on track for consolidation, with monetary policy likely taking on a larger role if growth teeters around or below the lower bound of Bank Negara's 4.0%-4.8% target range. Rates are expected to stay on hold in at the November meeting, with a potential cut in 1Q 2026, CIMB adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Nvidia Investment Puts Intel Back at Center of AI Ecosystem — Market Talk
data.description: Nvidia's $5 billion investment puts Intel back at the center of the AI ecosystem, Saxo chief investment strategist Charu Chanana says in a research note. For Intel to co-develop CPUs that plug directly into Nvidia's GPUs, its chips move from a supporting role to a core driver of performance, the strategist notes. Meanwhile, the deal gives Intel fresh credibility with customers and "plants seeds for Nvidia to eventually use Intel's foundry as a second manufacturing source if TSMC capacity becomes strained or geopolitics intervene," she adds. For Nvidia, the partnership provides "flexibility and reach," as it strengthens Nvidia's grip on AI data centers and opens the door to new categories of AI PCs, she says. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on Sept 19
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares are set to end a big central week in positive territory, buoyed by hopes of more monetary stimulus around the world, while the Nikkei notched a record aided by expectations the Bank of Japan would again skip a rate hike.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices were little changed on Friday after settling lower in the previous session, the day after the U.S. Federal Reserve cut interest rates for the first time this year, due to worries about fuel demand in the United States

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on South African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

The South African rand was largely flat after the central bank kept interest rates unchanged as it cautiously monitored the effect of previous cuts.

NIGERIA OIL

African petroleum regulators led by Nigeria have launched a continental forum aimed at harmonising oil regulation in a bid to attract crucial investment across the region's burgeoning energy sector, the Nigerian oil regulator said in a statement.

AFRICA CURRENCIES

The currencies of Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda and Zambia are expected to be broadly unchanged against the dollar in the next week to Thursday, traders said.

((Compiled by Nairobi Newsroom))

data.title: Top Midcap Gainers: JSW Infra, Motilal Oswal, NLC India stocks lead gains on Nifty Midcap 150 today
data.description:
At 9:30 am on Friday, several stocks were trading positively on the Nifty Midcap 150. Among the top gainers were JSW Infra, Motilal Oswal, and NLC India.

JSW Infra was trading at Rs 334.60, a gain of 2.11%. Motilal Oswal was trading at Rs 941.35, up 1.98%. NLC India was trading at Rs 259.65, higher by 1.36%. GE Vernova TD was trading at Rs 3,014.60, up 0.96%. Deepak Nitrite was trading at Rs 1,832.30, with a gain of 0.96%.

JSW Infrastructure Limited Financial Overview:

JSW Infrastructure Limited's revenue and net profit have shown substantial growth over the past five years. The consolidated revenue increased from Rs 1,603.57 Crore in 2021 to Rs 4,476.14 Crore in 2025. The net profit also rose significantly from Rs 284.62 Crore in 2021 to Rs 1,521.48 Crore in 2025.

data.title: Australia's Dyno Nobel rises; set for record best week in four
data.description:
** Shares of Australia's Dyno Nobel rise as much as 1.3% to A$3.02, set to record its best week since Aug 22, if trend holds

** Stock snaps three straight weeks of losses by rising 2.4% this week, as of 0417 GMT

** Brokerage Jefferies on Thursday raised price target on the fertiliser producer to A$3.15 from A$2.60 ahead of its Phosphate Hill plant review completion by September-end

** Stock has risen 2.1% this year, including session gains

data.title: Arfin India hits record high after order win
data.description:
** Shares of Arfin India rise 4.3% to a record high of 47 rupees

** Metal products maker wins orders worth 1.8 billion rupees ($20.40 million) from JFE Shoji India for supply of aluminium products

** Order for 6,900 MT to be executed over Oct 2025–Mar 2026, with 1,150 MT supplied monthly

** Stock up 10% since its debut on July 22

($1 = 88.2350 Indian rupees)

data.title: Nikkei Down 1.6% After BOJ Plan to Sell Japan Equity ETFs — Market Talk
data.description: Japan's Nikkei Stock Average is down 1.6% at 44562.63, giving up earlier gains, after the Bank of Japan says it will start selling its holdings of domestic equity exchange-traded funds gradually. While the BOJ kept its policy rate unchanged, the move shows the BOJ remains on course to tighten policy even as concerns have grown about the impact of U.S. tariffs on the global economy. The stock market is also weighed by a stronger yen following the BOJ policy decision. USD/JPY falls to 147.30 from 147.70 after the BOJ announcement. Investors will be focusing on Gov. Kazuo Ueda's press conference later Friday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Hyundai Motor India stock hits all-time high, rises 0.47%
data.description:
Shares of Hyundai Motor India hit an all-time high of Rs 2,740 on the NSE on Friday, rising 0.7% from the previous close. At 9:30 am, the automobile stock was trading at Rs 2,733.60. Hyundai Motor India is included as one of the stocks in the Nifty Next 50 index.

Corporate Actions:

The company announced a final dividend of Rs 21 per share (210%) with an effective date of August 5, 2025. Other corporate announcements include the signing of a long-term wage settlement as of September 17, 2025, and Regulation 30 disclosures as of September 1, 2025.Moneycontrol analysis indicates a very bullish sentiment on the stock as of September 19, 2025.

data.title: Australia's Peak Rare Earths hits over one-month high on court approval of takeover
data.description:
** Shares of Australia's Peak Rare Earths rise as much as 2.9% to A$0.443, their highest level since August 10

** Rare earths miner says the Supreme Court of New South Wales approved the takeover offer for co by Chinese rare earths producer Shenghe Resources , which already owns a 19.7% stake

** Under deal, Shenghe's Singapore unit will buy Peak's remaining shares for A$0.443 cash per share, valuing the miner at A$195 million ($128.93 million)

** Around 7 mln shares change hands, 2.8x the 30-day average trading volume

** Logs 1.2% in gains across the week, up for fourth consecutive week

** Stock last up 2.3%, bringing YTD gains to 318.1%

($1 = 1.5124 Australian dollars)
